resources.string: Needs specific number of steps to stop
Works in darkness and stops in light
Check your internet connection and try again
Displaying last locally saved prayer times
Check your internet connection and try again
Try selecting a different date or location
No prayer times available for this month
Error loading this month\'s prayer times: %1$s
Move your phone in a circle to calibrate the sensors
Reminder to fast on Mondays and Thursdays
Reminder to fast on the White Days (13, 14, 15)
Today is %1$s, may Allah bless this day 🤲
Tomorrow is Monday - Sunnah to fast 🌙
Tomorrow is Thursday - Sunnah to fast 🌙
Tomorrow is the %1$dth Hijri day - Sunnah to fast 🌙\n%2$s
To receive reminders for adhkar and prayer times
Find the "Seventy Times" app and enable it
Make sure all types of notifications are enabled
To show adhkar reminders while using other apps
Select "Appear on top of other apps"
Find "Seventy Times" and enable the option
To use light and motion sensors for smart reminders
Make sure to allow "Seventy Times" app
To ensure the app works in the background without interruption
These steps are necessary for the best experience with the app. Menu names may vary depending on the OS version.
Tomorrow is Monday. Reminder: Fasting on Mondays has great virtue.
Tomorrow is Thursday. Reminder: Fasting on Thursdays has great virtue.
Tomorrow is the %dth of the month. Reminder: Fasting the White Days.
Tomorrow is the Day of Arafah. Reminder: Fasting on this day has great virtue.
Tomorrow is the Day of Ashura. Reminder: Fasting on this day.
Tomorrow is the commemoration of the Prophet’s birthday.
Tomorrow is the commemoration of Isra and Mi\'raj.
Tomorrow is Laylat al-Qadr. Reminder to strive in worship.
Tomorrow is the first day of Ramadan. Congratulations!
Select your preferred sound for each prayer
• The alarm will ring daily at the set time • Music can only be stopped by completing the required steps • The alarm will be automatically scheduled for the next day
• The alarm will activate automatically in darkness • Music can only be stopped by exposure to light • It works throughout the day depending on ambient light
You can stop the alarm sound here if you cannot turn it off
Show a floating Azkar every 5 minutes over apps with sound
Please grant overlay permission to show Azkar reminders.
Reminder to send blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ
Movement alarm requires "Physical Activity" permission to accurately count your steps. Please enable this permission from app settings.
Tap to count • Long press for details
Your remembrances are your shield from all harm
Change your destination to %s? The travel mode will remain active with the new data.
No internet connection. Please check and try again.
An error occurred. Please try again.
The calculated distance is aerial. The Sharia ruling is based on the actual travel distance.

Only Dhuhr, ‘Asr, and ‘Isha’ are shortened (from 4 to 2). Fajr and Maghrib are not shortened. The prayer must be performed on time, not made up: if one makes up in travel a prayer missed while resident, one prays it completely; likewise if one makes up in residence a prayer missed while traveling, one also prays it completely.

Two stages or more = approximately 82 kilometers by the pace of loaded camels, a day and a night’s journey. The criterion is covering the distance, not time; if one covers it by plane or ship in an instant, shortening is permissible.

Obligatory, recommended, permissible, and disliked travel: shortening is allowed. Forbidden travel: no shortening. If a sinner repents during his journey and still has two or more stages ahead, shortening becomes permissible; otherwise not.

One must know that the distance is two stages or more. A wanderer who does not know his destination may not shorten. If one seeks a runaway slave without knowing his location, shortening is not allowed unless he knows that he will not find him except after two stages.
5 – Passing the built‑up area of the town

If the town has a wall → shortening begins as soon as one passes the wall (even if there is inhabited area outside it). If there is no wall → one must pass all inhabited areas, even if ruins lie in between. Farms, orchards, and graveyards beyond the town need not be passed. A Bedouin starts his journey by leaving the tents of his people, not just his own tent.
6 – Continuation of travel until the end of the prayer

If one intends to stay during the prayer or actually stays, one must complete the prayer. Intending to stay for 4 full days excluding the days of arrival and departure nullifies the concession from the moment of intention.
7 – Intention of shortening at the opening takbīr

The intention to shorten must be coupled with the opening takbīr with certainty. If it is delayed or precedes, completion is obligatory. (Al‑Muzanī: the intention may be made during the prayer. Abū Ḥanīfah: shortening is a resolution that does not require intention.)
8 – Not following a resident in any part of the prayer

If a traveler follows a resident in any part of the prayer, even in the final tashahhud or salām, completion becomes obligatory. If he is ignorant of the imām’s intention and attaches his intention to it, it is valid: if the imām shortens, he shortens; if he completes, he completes.

• Intends to stay during the prayer
• Doubts whether he intended to shorten
• Hesitates whether to complete or shorten
• Flees from his travel during the prayer

In cases other than those mentioned, completion is better for the traveler, to avoid disagreement. Example: a sailor who has his family with him on the ship; Imām Mālik does not allow him to shorten, so completion is preferable.

1) If the distance is ≥ 123 km (3 stages), to avoid Abū Ḥanīfah’s view that it is obligatory.
2) If one feels aversion to shortening.
3) If one doubts the evidence for allowing shortening.
4) If one is an example for people to follow.
Shortening is obligatory in one case
If time is too short to complete the prayer while traveling, shortening becomes obligatory, so that no part of the prayer leaves its time.
First scenario: Arrival at the homeland
As soon as he reaches the wall of his homeland (if it is walled) or its inhabited area (if not walled), his travel ends even if he does not enter.
Second scenario: Intention to stay 4 days

Intending to stay for 4 full days including their nights, excluding the day of arrival and departure, makes him a resident from the moment of intention. Evidence: “The emigrant may stay after completing his rites for three days” – three days do not make him a resident, more than that does. Residence is established anywhere, even in the desert.
Third scenario: Actual stay of 4 days
If he actually stays for 4 days excluding the days of arrival and departure, completion becomes obligatory even if he did not intend to stay, because actual stay takes precedence over intention.
Exception: One staying for a need awaiting completion

If he stays for a need that he expects to be fulfilled at any time and intends to leave as soon as it is done, he may shorten for up to 18 full days. Evidence: The Prophet ﷺ stayed during the campaign of Hawāzin for 18 days, shortening, awaiting the end of the war. If his need is delayed longer, he must complete.
📌 Rule for the day of arrival and departure

• The day of arrival and the day of departure are not counted among the four days.\n• If he enters at night, the rest of the night is not counted and the following day is counted.\n• Abū Ḥanīfah: 15 days (including arrival/departure) necessitate completion.
First category: The travel itself is sinful

He initiated the travel for a sinful purpose from the outset (e.g., traveling to steal or drink alcohol). He may not shorten. If he repents and still has two or more stages ahead, shortening becomes permissible; otherwise not.
Second category: His travel becomes sinful mid‑way

He initiated the travel for a permissible purpose then turned it into a sin. If he repents, shortening is permissible absolutely, regardless of the remaining distance, because the origin of his travel was permissible.
He initiated permissible travel and a sin occurred incidentally during it. He is not prevented from shortening, because no one is free from sin.
Abū Ḥanīfah, al‑Thawrī, al‑Awzā‘ī, and al‑Muzanī: a sinner may avail himself of all travel concessions. The relied‑upon position in the Shāfi‘ī school: no concession for a sinful journey.
Begin with the first: Dhuhr before ‘Asr, Maghrib before ‘Ishā’. If reversed: the first is valid, the second counts as a voluntary prayer for one who is ignorant or forgetful.
Second condition: Intention of combining before finishing the first

One intends to combine in advance during the first prayer, even with its salām. It is best to make the intention with the opening takbīr. (Al‑Muzanī: intention is not required. The relied‑upon position: it is required.)\nDifference from shortening: in shortening, the intention must be coupled with the takbīr, because delaying it would perform part of the prayer in its complete form.

There should not be a long customary gap between the two prayers. The benchmark: less than two light rak‘ahs according to some. A slight delay for a person performing tayammum is excused because he needs a new tayammum for the second prayer.
Fourth condition: Continuation of travel until the opening of the second
The excuse of travel must persist until the end of the opening takbīr of the second prayer. If he settles or intends to settle before that, combining is not valid.
Caveats: When it becomes invalid or delay becomes obligatory

• If he prays the second before the first → the second is invalid.
• If he did not intend to combine in the first → the second must be delayed to its time.
• If he separates them by a long gap → the second must be delayed.
• If he settles before starting the second → it must be delayed.
• If he settles after finishing both or during the second → they remain valid.
The sunnah before Dhuhr, then the two obligatory prayers, then the sunnah after Dhuhr, then the sunnah of ‘Asr. It is not allowed to offer the sunnah of the second before the two obligatory prayers.
First condition: Intention of delay before the time of the first expires

He intends to delay the first to the time of the second before the time of the first expires. Al‑Ramli and al‑Khaṭīb: the time for intention extends until enough time remains to perform the entire prayer. Ibn Ḥajar: it suffices that enough time remains for one rak‘ah. If he delays the first without intending to combine, he sins and it counts as a make‑up.
Second condition: Continuation of travel until both prayers are completed
Travel must persist until the end of the second prayer. If he settles before performing them, the combination is invalid and the follower becomes a make‑up with no sin.
Difference from advance: Order and consecutiveness are recommended

In delay combining:
• Order (first before second): recommended, not obligatory.
• Consecutiveness: recommended, not obligatory.
• Intention in the first: recommended.
Advance: from the takbīr to the salām of the first
Delay: from the entry of the first’s time until enough time remains to perform it
Advance: until the completion of the takbīr of the second
Delay: until the completion of both prayers entirely
Which is better for the traveler?

• Traveling during the first’s time and staying during the second’s → delay is better (easier for him).\n• Staying during the first’s time and traveling during the second’s → advance is better.\n• Staying during both times or traveling during both → disagreement: Ibn Ḥajar: advance is better (discharge of responsibility). Al‑Ramli: delay is better (the second’s time is also a time for the first).
Fasting is allowed if the distance reaches the Shar‘ī limit and he leaves his town before dawn. Fasting is better if it does not harm him.
He must make up the number of days he missed. There is no expiation for a traveler who breaks his fast.
If he starts fasting then travels
He is allowed to break his fast on that day.
Traveler: 3 days and nights (72 hours). Resident: one day and night (24 hours).
Start of calculation and conditions of wearing
The duration is counted from the first wipe after an impurity, not from the time of wearing. The khuff must be put on in a state of complete purity.
Major impurity (janābah), removing the khuff, or expiry of the period.
Every 3 Ḥawqalah strengthen a new branch
Remember Allah to grow your tree
May Allah bless you, you reached level %d
Continue with dhikr to let your tree grow, bloom, and bear fruit
All your points, dhikr counts, and level will be permanently deleted. This action cannot be undone.
Your daily companion for dhikr and worship
An Islamic app designed to be with you at all times,\nreminding you of prayer times, morning and evening adhkār,\nand helping you revive daily sunan and acts of worship with ease.
Let us show you what you can do 👇
A comprehensive collection of morning and evening adhkār,\nadhkār for sleeping and waking up, with the required count\nfor each dhikr and its virtue.
💡 Tap a dhikr to count, long‑press to see details
Stay updated with the Islamic calendar
Displays the current Hijri date accurately,\nwith Islamic occasions and virtuous days\nsuch as the White Days, ‘Āshūrā’, and others.
💡 The date updates automatically every day
Set your city once for accurate prayer times and Qibla
Set your geographical location once\nso the app calculates prayer times and Qibla direction\nwith high accuracy for any city in the world.
⚠️ Important: start by setting your location for all other services to work correctly
The five daily prayer times are calculated accurately\nbased on your location, with customizable notifications\nand a full monthly schedule.
💡 Enable notifications in settings to be reminded before each prayer
An elegant digital tasbīḥ that counts your glorifications,\nallows you to set a goal and reset,\nand saves your daily total.
💡 Tap anywhere on the screen to count
Know the direction of the Kaaba wherever you are
An accurate compass showing the Qibla direction\nbased on your current location, with the\ndistance to Makkah.
Turn your dhikr into growing reward
Visualize the reward of your remembrance in a beautiful way;\nevery time you remember Allah, your tree grows\nand your reward increases, by Allah’s will.
💡 One dhikr may weigh mountains — do not underestimate
Adhkār and rulings for the traveler
Your comprehensive travel guide: travel adhkār,\nrulings on shortening and combining prayers,\nand Islamic travel etiquette.
💡 Save it before your journey to view offline
Plant in your garden every day

The four phrases most beloved to Allah:
Subḥānallāh, walḥamdulillāh,
walā ilāha illallāh, wallāhu akbar.
Consistently recite them and track your progress.
💡 “Two words light on the tongue, heavy in the scale”
O Allah, accept from us — wird #%d
«Let your tongue remain moist with the remembrance of Allah»
اللَّهُمَّ صَلِّ وَسَلِّمْ عَلَى نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ ﷺ
لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ لَا شَرِيكَ لَهُ، لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَلَهُ الْحَمْدُ وَهُوَ عَلَى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ
لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللَّهِ الْعَلِيِّ الْعَظِيمِ
حَسْبِيَ اللَّهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا هُوَ عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَهُوَ رَبُّ الْعَرْشِ الْعَظِيمِ
لا حول ولا قوة إلا بالله
Type the city name in any language
✅ Shortening, combining and breaking fast are permitted
❌ The distance (%d km approx.) is below the legal limit (%d km)
Travel mode will remain active with the new data.
It is allowed to break the fast if the distance reaches the Shar‘ī limit and he leaves his town before dawn. Fasting is better if it does not harm him.
He must make up the number of days he missed. There is no expiation for a traveler who breaks his fast.
If he starts fasting then travels
He is allowed to break his fast on that day.
